speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Go tell the lords o’ th’ city I am here.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Deliver them this paper.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Having read it,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Bid them repair to th’ marketplace, where I,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Even in theirs and in the commons’ ears,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Will vouch the truth of it. Him I accuse

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: The city ports by this hath entered and

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Intends t’ appear before the people, hoping

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: To purge himself with words. Dispatch.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Most welcome!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: How is it with our general?

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Even so

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: As with a man by his own alms empoisoned

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: And with his charity slain.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: Most noble sir,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: If you do hold the same intent wherein

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: You wished us parties, we’ll deliver you

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: Of your great danger.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Sir, I cannot tell.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: We must proceed as we do find the people.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: The people will remain uncertain whilst

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: ’Twixt you there’s difference, but the fall of either

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: Makes the survivor heir of all.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: I know it,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: And my pretext to strike at him admits

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: A good construction. I raised him, and I pawned

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Mine honor for his truth, who, being so heightened,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: He watered his new plants with dews of flattery,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Seducing so my friends; and to this end,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: He bowed his nature, never known before

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: But to be rough, unswayable, and free.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: Sir, his stoutness

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: When he did stand for consul, which he lost

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: By lack of stooping—

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: That I would have spoke of.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Being banished for ’t, he came unto my hearth,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Presented to my knife his throat. I took him,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Made him joint servant with me, gave him way

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: In all his own desires; nay, let him choose

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Out of my files, his projects to accomplish,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: My best and freshest men; served his designments

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: In mine own person; holp to reap the fame

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Which he did end all his; and took some pride

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: To do myself this wrong; till at the last

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: I seemed his follower, not partner; and

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: He waged me with his countenance as if

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: I had been mercenary.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: So he did, my lord.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: The army marvelled at it, and, in the last,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: When he had carried Rome and that we looked

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: For no less spoil than glory—

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: There was it

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: For which my sinews shall be stretched upon him.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: At a few drops of women’s rheum, which are

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: As cheap as lies, he sold the blood and labor

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Of our great action. Therefore shall he die,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: And I’ll renew me in his fall. But hark!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: Your native town you entered like a post

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: And had no welcomes home, but he returns

speaker:
FIRST
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: Splitting the air with noise.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: And patient fools,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: Whose children he hath slain, their base throats tear

speaker:
SECOND
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: With giving him glory.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: Therefore at your vantage,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: Ere he express himself or move the people

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: With what he would say, let him feel your sword,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: Which we will second. When he lies along,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: After your way his tale pronounced shall bury

speaker:
THIRD
               
              CONSPIRATOR
text: His reasons with his body.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Say no more.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Here come the lords.

speaker:
ALL
               
              LORDS
text: You are most welcome home.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: I have not deserved it.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: But, worthy lords, have you with heed perused

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: What I have written to you?

speaker:
ALL
               
              LORDS
text: We have.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: And grieve to hear ’t.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: What faults he made before the last, I think

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Might have found easy fines, but there to end

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Where he was to begin and give away

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: The benefit of our levies, answering us

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: With our own charge, making a treaty where

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: There was a yielding—this admits no excuse.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: He approaches. You shall hear him.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Hail, lords! I am returned your soldier,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: No more infected with my country’s love

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Than when I parted hence, but still subsisting

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Under your great command. You are to know

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That prosperously I have attempted, and

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: With bloody passage led your wars even to

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The gates of Rome. Our spoils we have brought home

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Doth more than counterpoise a full third part

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The charges of the action. We have made peace

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: With no less honor to the Antiates

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Than shame to th’ Romans, and we here deliver,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Subscribed by’ th’ Consuls and patricians,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Together with the seal o’ th’ Senate, what

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: We have compounded on.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Read it not, noble lords,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: But tell the traitor in the highest degree

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: He hath abused your powers.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: “Traitor”? How now?

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Ay, traitor, Martius.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Martius?

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Ay, Martius, Caius Martius. Dost thou think

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: I’ll grace thee with that robbery, thy stol’n name

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Coriolanus, in Corioles?

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: You lords and heads o’ th’ state, perfidiously

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: He has betrayed your business and given up

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: For certain drops of salt your city Rome—

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: I say your city—to his wife and mother,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Breaking his oath and resolution like

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: A twist of rotten silk, never admitting

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Counsel o’ th’ war, but at his nurse’s tears

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: He whined and roared away your victory,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: That pages blushed at him and men of heart

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Looked wond’ring each at other.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Hear’st thou, Mars?

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Name not the god, thou boy of tears.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Ha?

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: No more.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Measureless liar, thou hast made my heart

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Too great for what contains it. “Boy”? O slave!—

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Pardon me, lords, ’tis the first time that ever

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I was forced to scold. Your judgments, my grave lords,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Must give this cur the lie; and his own notion—

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Who wears my stripes impressed upon him, that

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Must bear my beating to his grave—shall join

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To thrust the lie unto him.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Peace, both, and hear me speak.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Cut me to pieces, Volsces. Men and lads,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Stain all your edges on me. “Boy”? False hound!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: If you have writ your annals true, ’tis there

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That like an eagle in a dovecote, I

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Fluttered your Volscians in Corioles,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Alone I did it. “Boy”!

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Why, noble lords,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Will you be put in mind of his blind fortune,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Which was your shame, by this unholy braggart,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: ’Fore your own eyes and ears?

speaker:
ALL
               
              CONSPIRATORS
text: Let him die for ’t.

speaker:
ALL
               
              PEOPLE
text: Tear him to pieces! Do it presently! He

speaker:
ALL
               
              PEOPLE
text: killed my son! My daughter! He killed my cousin

speaker:
ALL
               
              PEOPLE
text: Marcus! He killed my father!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: Peace, ho! No outrage! Peace!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: The man is noble, and his fame folds in

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: This orb o’ th’ Earth. His last offenses to us

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: Shall have judicious hearing. Stand, Aufidius,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: And trouble not the peace.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: O, that I had him,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: With six Aufidiuses, or more, his tribe,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To use my lawful sword.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Insolent villain!

speaker:
ALL
               
              CONSPIRATORS
text: Kill, kill, kill, kill, kill him!

speaker: LORDS
text: Hold, hold, hold, hold!

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: My noble masters, hear me speak.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: O Tullus!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: Thou hast done a deed whereat valor will weep.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              LORD
text: Tread not upon him.—Masters, all be quiet.—

speaker:
THIRD
               
              LORD
text: Put up your swords.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: My lords, when you shall know—as in this rage,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Provoked by him, you cannot—the great danger

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Which this man’s life did owe you, you’ll rejoice

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: That he is thus cut off. Please it your Honors

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: To call me to your senate, I’ll deliver

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Myself your loyal servant or endure

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Your heaviest censure.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Bear from hence his body,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: And mourn you for him. Let him be regarded

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: As the most noble corse that ever herald

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Did follow to his urn.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: His own impatience

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: Takes from Aufidius a great part of blame.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: Let’s make the best of it.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: My rage is gone,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: And I am struck with sorrow.—Take him up.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Help, three o’ th’ chiefest soldiers; I’ll be one.—

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Beat thou the drum that it speak mournfully.—

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Trail your steel pikes. Though in this city he

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Hath widowed and unchilded many a one,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Which to this hour bewail the injury,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Yet he shall have a noble memory.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Assist.
